title: WorldГўв‚¬в„ўs Largest SOEC Electrolyzer Started up at NesteГўв‚¬в„ўs Rotterdam Refinery Demonstrating Viability of Renewable Hydrogen
content: The pioneering MultiPLHY project demonstrating renewable hydrogen production has reached a key milestone: The consortium partners have successfully started up the worldГўв‚¬в„ўs largest multi-megawatt high-temperature electrolyzer (HTE) in an industrial environment at NesteГўв‚¬в„ўs renewable products refinery in Rotterdam, the Netherlands. The pilot project demonstrates the viability of renewable hydrogen in reducing the use of fossil hydrogen in the refining industry. Replacing hydrogen produced from fossil raw materials with renewable hydrogen is one of the key means to lower greenhouse gas emissions in refining. As a next step in the demonstration project, a test program will validate the technologyГўв‚¬в„ўs performance characteristics. MultiPLHY is a demonstration project with consortium partners Neste, Sunfire, CEA, and ENGIE. The high-temperature electrolyzer is provided by the German electrolyzer manufacturer Sunfire and the hydrogen processing unit (HPU) by SMS group. Neste is responsible for the refinery integration and together with Sunfire oversees the operation of the unit. The research and technology organization CEA coordinates the project and ENGIE is in charge of techno-economic assessment.
author: Analysts Account
